media.name: r-RSM (renneted reconstituted skim milk gel + chymosin) (PMC4322847)

current_components.name: chloride (native ~28 mM + ~20 mM from added CaCl2)

current_uncovered: casein (bovine, whole; alpha-s1, alpha-s2, beta, kappa)
whey proteins (beta-lactoglobulin, alpha-lactalbumin, serum albumin, immunoglobulins)

free amino acids (glutamic acid/glutamine most abundant, 46% of total free AA; plus the full set of casein-derived residues: proline, leucine, lysine, aspartate, valine, isoleucine, phenylalanine, tyrosine, threonine, serine, alanine, arginine, glycine, histidine, methionine, tryptophan, cysteine, asparagine)
calcium (native colloidal + soluble) plus 10 mM CaCl2 added
inorganic phosphate (as total P, colloidal calcium phosphate + soluble)
media.base_medium: Reconstituted skim milk (RSM): 10% (w/v) low-heat skim milk powder in distilled water, supplemented with 10 mM CaCl2, heat-treated 90 C/30 min. r-RSM = same medium coagulated with chymosin (rennet) added at inoculation.
media.base_medium_reference: Stulova I, Kabanova N, Krisciunaite T, Adamberg K, Laht T-M, Vilu R. Microcalorimetric study of the growth of Streptococcus thermophilus in renneted milk. Front Microbiol. 2015;6:79. doi:10.3389/fmicb.2015.00079 (PMC4322847). Skim-milk constituent concentrations (native milk salts, protein, vitamins) not tabulated in the paper; taken from standard dairy-composition references: MilkFacts.info nutrient content of milk; Gaucheron F. The minerals of milk. Reprod Nutr Dev. 2005;45:473-483 (https://rnd.edpsciences.org/articles/rnd/pdf/2005/04/r5402.pdf); Dairy Global Nutrition, Nutritional composition of skim milk powder.
media.verification_evidence: "Milk gels were prepared by addition of 100 μl of a 10 g L−1 chymosin (CHY-MAX Powder Extra, Hørsholm, Denmark) aqueous solution to 10 mL of RSM. The enzyme (chymosin) was added immediately after the inoculation." Base RSM: "LHSMP was suspended in distilled water to yield a final concentration of 10% (w v−1) milk solids, with addition of CaCl2 in final concentration of 10 mM."
media.citation: Medium composition from PMC4322847 (see GrowthDB record).

media.name: RSM (liquid reconstituted skim milk) (PMC4322847)

current_uncovered: casein (bovine, whole; alpha-s1, alpha-s2, beta, kappa)
whey proteins (beta-lactoglobulin, alpha-lactalbumin, serum albumin, immunoglobulins)

free amino acids (glutamic acid/glutamine most abundant, 46% of total free AA; plus the full set of casein-derived residues: proline, leucine, lysine, aspartate, valine, isoleucine, phenylalanine, tyrosine, threonine, serine, alanine, arginine, glycine, histidine, methionine, tryptophan, cysteine, asparagine)
calcium (native colloidal + soluble) plus 10 mM CaCl2 added
inorganic phosphate (as total P, colloidal calcium phosphate + soluble)
media.base_medium: Reconstituted skim milk (RSM): 10% (w/v) low-heat skim milk powder in distilled water, supplemented with 10 mM CaCl2, heat-treated 90 C/30 min. r-RSM = same medium coagulated with chymosin (rennet) added at inoculation.

Front Microbiol. 2015;6:79. doi:10.3389/fmicb.2015.00079 (PMC4322847). Skim-milk constituent concentrations (native milk salts, protein, vitamins) not tabulated in the paper; taken from standard dairy-composition references: MilkFacts.info nutrient content of milk; Gaucheron F. media.verification_evidence: "LHSMP was suspended in distilled water to yield a final concentration of 10% (w v−1) milk solids, with addition of CaCl2 in final concentration of 10 mM." Milk was then "heated at 90°C for 30 min and cooled to 30°C immediately before the experiments."
media.citation: Medium composition from PMC4322847 (see GrowthDB record).
